mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with low federal funds rates creates a favorable liquidity and demand environment that amplifies equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline with t-test p > 0.05 or produces negative returns in >60% of cases when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and robust economic activity, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recovery phases or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained economic growth and consumer spending power, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in <50% of months with payroll gains or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.0 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive returns in >60% of time during high inflation periods or the threshold fails to predict direction with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production signals broad-based economic strength and capital investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >55% of occurrences or interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.5 percent during late-cycle phases signals economic overheating and impending policy tightening, which suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High GDP growth consistently predicts positive returns in late-cycle regimes or the signal predictive accuracy remains below Rank IC 0.03 across business cycle phases.
